communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Any communications (including emails + text messages + records of video calls over Zoom, Google Meet, etc + records of phone calls) between anyone at the Texas State Comptroller's office and anyone at Chevron (email domain: @chevron.com) or any outside parties, such as lawyers, representing the company between July 1, 2025 and the date this request is processed.
2) Any informational documents, memoranda, agreements, etc. mentioning Chevron's JETI application for Energy Forge One, LLC (J0022, Pecos-Barstow-Toyah ISD, posted 11/10/25)
3) Any internal communications (including emails + text messages + records of video calls over Zoom, Google Meet, etc + records of phone calls) between staff at the Texas State Comptroller's office regarding Chevron's JETI application for Energy Forge One, LLC (J0022, Pecos-Barstow-Toyah ISD, posted 11/10/25)

In order to help to determine my status to assess fees, you should know that I am a journalist for WIRED magazine. Disclosure of the requested information to me is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations or activities of the government and is not primarily in my commercial interest.

In the interest of time, I request that you provide documents as they become available rather than waiting for the complete set.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Molly Taft

title: Open Records Request — Georgia AG / Flock Safety Communications

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request — Georgia AG / Flock Safety Communications
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All emails, text messages, direct messages, or other written communications between any employee or representative of the Office of the Attorney General and Bob Carter (Vice President, Flock Safety) from April 3, 2026 through April 13, 2026.

All emails, text messages, direct messages, or other written communications between any employee or representative of the Office of the Attorney General and any Flock Safety employee or representative from April 3, 2026 through April 13, 2026.

Any internal communications within the Office of the Attorney General referencing Flock Safety, Bob Carter, or the LinkedIn post published on or about April 11, 2026 from the Attorney General's official LinkedIn account, from April 3, 2026 through April 13, 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar
